definition: The Treasury basis trade ($800B-$1.5T at 20-100x leverage) is a systemic fragility with no historical parallel that meets Minsky's exact Ponzi finance criterion: operating cash flows (5-15bp basis spread) are insufficient to service debt obligations when financing conditions tighten even marginally. The structure depends entirely on repo availability, basis stability, and manageable quarter-end disruptions.
evidence: March 2020 required $1.6T in emergency Fed purchases at lower notional
Meets Minsky Ponzi finance criterion precisely
SEC clearing mandate (Q2 2026) could force 30-50% leverage compression with zero event premium currently priced in SOFR futures
open_questions: What is the activation probability given Knightian uncertainty (rated 2/10)?
Will the SEC clearing mandate be implemented on schedule or face delays?
How effective would Fed transitional liquidity facilities be in preventing cascading unwinding?
What is the actual current notional and leverage distribution?
